remember: Students will be able to recall the types of reconstitution of partnership and the process of admission of a new partner.
Students will be able to remember the rights of the new partner and the existing partners after admission.
Students will be able to recall the methods of valuation of goodwill on admission.
understand: Students will be able to understand the need for reconstitution of partnership and the effects of admission of a new partner on the firm and its partners.
Students will be able to understand the different methods of valuation of goodwill and their advantages and disadvantages.
Students will be able to understand the role of a partner's capital in the firm and its impact on the admission process.
apply: Students will be able to apply the concepts of reconstitution of partnership and admission of a new partner to real-life scenarios.
Students will be able to apply the methods of valuation of goodwill and calculate the amount of goodwill to be brought in by the new partner.
Students will be able to apply the principles of partnership to determine the rights and interests of the new and existing partners.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the effects of admission of a new partner on the firm's capital structure and the balance sheet.
Students will be able to analyze the different methods of valuation of goodwill and recommend the most suitable method for a given scenario.
Students will be able to analyze the impact of a new partner's capital on the firm's profits and losses.

ncert_objectives: Explain the need for reconstitution of partnership and the effects of admission of a new partner on the firm and its partners.
Describe the methods of valuation of goodwill and calculate the amount of goodwill to be brought in by the new partner.
Determine the rights and interests of the new and existing partners after admission.
board_exam_objectives: To calculate the new partner's share of profits and losses.
To determine the amount of goodwill to be brought in by the new partner.
To analyze the effects of admission of a new partner on the firm's capital structure and the balance sheet.
